basic_details.job_title: BFUHS Medical Officer (General) Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 630 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot
basic_details.department: Department of Health and Family Welfare, Punjab
basic_details.short_information: Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot has invited online applications for 630 Medical Officer (General) posts under the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Punjab. This recruitment is issued vide Advertisement No. BFU-26/26. Eligible candidates holding an MBBS degree can apply online from 16 July 2026 to 5 August 2026. Selection will be through a written examination conducted by BFUHS. Here are the complete details of eligibility, vacancy breakup, age limit, salary, fee and how to apply.

application_fee.details: The application fee is non-refundable. Mode of fee payment is not specified in the notification.

age_limit.details: Age limit: between 18 and 37 years as on 1 January 2026.

qualification.details: MBBS Degree Registered with the Punjab Medical Council or with any duly constituted Medical Council in India Knowledge of Punjabi Language up to Matriculation Standard or its equivalent. Candidates who do not possess this qualification must acquire it within six months of joining the service, failing which their services will be terminated | A candidate must be one of the following: | A citizen of India, or A citizen of Nepal, or A subject of Bhutan, or A Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanent settlement, or A person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or the East African countries of Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania (formerly Tanganyika and Zanzibar), Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia or Vietnam with the intention of permanently settling in India | Candidates falling under the Nepal, Bhutan, Tibetan refugee, or migrant categories above must hold an eligibility certificate issued by the Government of Punjab, Department of Home Affairs and Justice.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be given pay as per the central pattern for new recruits under the 7th CPC, with initial pay as per the pay matrix table at Level 9, amounting to ₹53,100/-.

selection_process: Not specified: whether a separate interview or document verification round follows the written exam.
how_to_apply: Visit the official BFUHS website: www.bfuhs.ac.in
Locate the recruitment notification for Medical Officer (General), Advt. No. BFU-26/26
Read the detailed notification carefully for category-wise vacancy, criteria and terms and conditions
Fill out the online application form with correct personal, educational and category details
Mention your sub-category/caste accurately in the application form, as requested
Pay the applicable non-refundable application fee (₹2,360/- for all categories except SC; ₹1,180/- for SC category)
Submit the application within the window: 16 July 2026 to 5 August 2026

content_sections.heading: BFUHS Medical Officer Recruitment 2026: Quick Overview

rows.details: Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot
Department of Health and Family Welfare, Punjab

content_sections.heading: BFUHS Medical Officer Vacancy Details: Category-wise Breakup
content_sections.text: The 630 vacancies include 540 fresh posts and 90 backlog posts, distributed across categories as follows. Note: the source notification lists the "Total" row as 540 fresh, 90 backlog and 630 combined, which matches the sum of the individual category rows above.

content_sections.heading: BFUHS Medical Officer Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: MBBS Degree Registered with the Punjab Medical Council or with any duly constituted Medical Council in India Knowledge of Punjabi Language up to Matriculation Standard or its equivalent. Candidates who do not possess this qualification must acquire it within six months of joining the service, failing which their services will be terminated A candidate must be one of the following: A citizen of India, or A citizen of Nepal, or A subject of Bhutan, or A Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanent settlement, or A person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or the East African countries of Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania (formerly Tanganyika and Zanzibar), Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia or Vietnam with the intention of permanently settling in India Candidates falling under the Nepal, Bhutan, Tibetan refugee, or migrant categories above must hold an eligibility certificate issued by the Government of Punjab, Department of Home Affairs and Justice.

content_sections.heading: BFUHS Medical Officer Age Limit
content_sections.text: Age limit: between 18 and 37 years as on 1 January 2026.

rows.age_relaxation: Actual service period in the Armed Forces is deducted from actual age; if the resultant age does not exceed the prescribed maximum by more than 3 years, the candidate is deemed to satisfy the age condition

rows.category: Punjab Govt./Board/Corporation/Commission/Authority employees, and all State/Central Govt. employees
Widows, Divorcees and certain other categories of Women

content_sections.heading: BFUHS Medical Officer Salary
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be given pay as per the central pattern for new recruits under the 7th CPC, with initial pay as per the pay matrix table at Level 9 , amounting to ₹53,100/- .

content_sections.heading: BFUHS Medical Officer Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee is non-refundable. Mode of fee payment is not specified in the notification.

content_sections.heading: BFUHS Medical Officer Selection Process
content_sections.text: Not specified: whether a separate interview or document verification round follows the written exam.

content_sections.heading: BFUHS Medical Officer Required Documents
content_sections.text: Certificates/diplomas/degrees establishing MBBS qualification (only those from recognized institutions are considered) Punjab Medical Council / duly constituted Medical Council registration proof Category certificate (SC/BC/EWS, etc.) issued by the Competent Authority, as applicable Ex-Serviceman/LDESM/Gallantry Award descendant certificate with Punjab domicile, as applicable Sports Gradation Certificate issued by the Director Sports, Punjab, for candidates claiming Sports quota benefit (must be held as on the last date of online application submission) Eligibility certificate from the Punjab Government, Department of Home Affairs and Justice, for candidates under the Nepal/Bhutan/Tibetan refugee/migrant nationality categories Police Verification certificate and clearance of any pending criminal charges (required at the appointment stage, not at the exam stage)

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for BFUHS Medical Officer Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official BFUHS website: www.bfuhs.ac.in Locate the recruitment notification for Medical Officer (General), Advt. No. BFU-26/26 Read the detailed notification carefully for category-wise vacancy, criteria and terms and conditions Fill out the online application form with correct personal, educational and category details Mention your sub-category/caste accurately in the application form, as requested Pay the applicable non-refundable application fee (₹2,360/- for all categories except SC; ₹1,180/- for SC category) Submit the application within the window: 16 July 2026 to 5 August 2026

faq.question: Q1. When was the BFUHS Medical Officer Recruitment 2026 notification released?
faq.answer: Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS) has released the recruitment advertisement for Medical Officer posts in 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the post name under BFUHS Medical Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The post name is Medical Officer.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in BFUHS Medical Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 630 vacancies (540 fresh and 90 backlog) for the post of Medical Officer (General).

faq.question: Q4. What is the last date to apply for BFUHS Medical Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 5 August 2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the qualification required for BFUHS Medical Officer post?
faq.answer: Candidates need an MBBS degree and registration with the Punjab Medical Council or any duly constituted Medical Council in India.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for BFUHS Medical Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 37 years as on 1 January 2026, with age relaxation applicable for reserved categories as specified in the notification.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for BFUHS Medical Officer 2026?
faq.answer: ₹2,360/- for all categories except SC, and ₹1,180/- for SC category.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary of a BFUHS Medical Officer?
faq.answer: Initial pay is ₹53,100/- per the 7th CPC pay matrix at Level 9.

faq.question: Q9. Is knowledge of Punjabi mandatory for this post?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates need knowledge of Punjabi up to Matriculation standard or equivalent, or must acquire it within six months of joining, failing which their services will be terminated.

faq.question: Q10. What is the minimum qualifying mark in the BFUHS Medical Officer written exam?
faq.answer: Candidates must score at least 33% marks in the written test to be declared eligible.
